response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with strong full-stack, testing, and Linux experience, plus relevant geospatial work via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as a primary language, and the resume shows only limited, dated C/C++ exposure (National Instruments, 2014-2015) and no Rust. Core experience is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, neither of which substitutes for production C++/Rust skill in a routing tiles pipeline. Worth a screen only if they can demonstrate transferable systems-level depth.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map projects
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, low-latency services, distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic
Long senior/staff track record with cross-stack adaptability and Linux server work
concerns: No demonstrated modern C++ experience; C/C++ work is over a decade old and limited in scope
Recent roles centered on TypeScript/Node.js product work, not routing/tile pipelines or systems programming

key_factors.factor: Senior systems & distributed-systems engineering track record
